chapter_title: Prevention of head lice in schools
snippet: Although the responsibility for detecting and treating head lice rests with parents, it is important that practical advice and support be available. Schools are encouraged to work collaboratively with parents and the broader school community to implement practices and procedures to prevent and minimise the impact of head lice outbreaks. Strategies that schools can implement to prevent the transmission of head lice are listed below. It is considered best practice to: Head lice do not live or breed on furniture, carpets, clothes or soft toys and cannot be spread through sharing items of clothing, for example, hats.
